title: viola crassa var. borealis
intro: Viola crassa var. borealis, also known as the Arctic pansy or northern viola, is a rare alpine plant species found in the Arctic tundra regions of North America and Greenland. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Inuit medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat colds, fever, and respiratory problems due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can also help to reduce swelling and improve circulation. Herbal preparations of Viola crassa var. borealis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, salves, and other topical treatments for a range of health benefits.
